name: SAN ACADEMY - Pallikaranai

itemListElement.reviewBody:
Disappointing Experience – Not the Right Environment for Young Children

I enrolled my child in this school hoping for a supportive and nurturing environment, but unfortunately, our experience has been extremely disappointing.

As part of my child’s daily snack routine, I pack fruits, nuts/seeds, and a small milk rusk. Two weeks ago, my child came home upset, saying he was being bullied by classmates for bringing “junk food.” To my shock, the teacher had reportedly encouraged the entire class to give a "thumbs down" gesture to any child bringing such items. I find this method completely inappropriate and humiliating. Promoting healthy eating is important, but it must be done respectfully — not by shaming a child in front of peers.

I raised this concern with the school. One coordinator told me that I felt different because I was new to the school and explained their practice of giving smiley or sad stickers based on the child’s snacks. I was open to this approach, as it can help parents track progress in a constructive way. However, the public “thumbs down” action is unacceptable. Despite multiple attempts to explain my concern, the staff consistently defended the teacher and refused to acknowledge any wrongdoing.

The principal, instead of addressing the issue, asked me what the school should do if students bring junk food. As a parent, I expect the school leadership to know how to foster healthy habits respectfully — that’s not the parent's job to dictate. Furthermore, if there were concerns about the rusk in my child's snack box, they could have easily informed me — either in person or through the school diary. Sadly, nothing was communicated until this incident of public shaming occurred.

Adding to my concern, I’ve observed that the overall quality of teaching is below expectation. Teachers often write lessons on the board for students to copy without actually explaining the content. When I asked my child how a math problem was solved, he said, “Ma’am just wrote it on the board.” I’ve heard similar complaints from other parents.

Moreover, the medium of instruction is not consistently in English. Teachers often speak only in Tamil, which is disappointing in a school that claims to follow CBSE standards. Ironically, while the school insists on healthy snacks, during events like annual day, they themselves provide biscuits and sugary drinks like Maaza to the children.

In a separate incident, when my child requested to use the restroom, a teacher used the word “pae” — a disrespectful and inappropriate term in Tamil. I reported this as well, but again, it was brushed off.

What’s most frustrating is the lack of accountability. The school seems more focused on collecting fees and putting up a show for new admissions, rather than delivering on quality education or student welfare. Public appreciation can build confidence — but public humiliation can cause lasting harm.

After the incident, I immediately withdrew my child from the school. Children deserve kindness, respect, and a safe learning environment. Sadly, this school failed to provide that.

This is for san Academy Pallikaranai for Grade 1

As part of his snack routine, I pack fruits and a mix of nuts/seeds, along with a small milk rusk to encourage him to eat everything. Two weeks ago, he told me he was being bullied for bringing “junk food.” To my surprise, the teacher has  asked the whole class to do a "thumbs down" gesture at any child who brings such items.

I found this very inappropriate. Instead of encouraging healthy eating in a respectful way, this method shames the child. A teacher can either speak privately to the student or inform the parents — involving the whole class in such gestures is not the right approach.

I raised a complaint and also met the principal. Sadly, the justification given was, “We appreciate students in front of everyone, so this should be taken the same way. I strongly disagree. Public appreciation boosts confidence, but public humiliation can deeply affect a child.

Earlier too, when Renato asked teacher to use the restroom, the teacher used an inappropriate word (“pae” in Tamil), which I also reported. 

I’m disappointed with the way such situations are being handled. Our children deserve kindness, respect, and a safe learning space.

My Kid studied here from Sr.KG to till 10. We changed the school because of the irresponsible behaviour school management. The school was good till covid and after covid they became money minded. Suddenly they increased the Transport Fees from 2500 to 3800 per month(5 KM Distance). During board exams we have to make our own arrangements to drop and pick up the kids even though they have collected the Transport fees for the same. When enquired there is no response from the school. Very very worst management.
Highly partial in awarding in co curricular activities. Not following the rules or favouring a particular school of their...

School used to be good a couple of years ago but is degraded by its quality of education and other activities. Everything is name sake. School is becoming business oriented and class strength is unprecedented and kids are learning nothing meaningful. If Parents don't put effort it's a gamble we parents take on kids education. BTW 2 of my kids are studying here and it's my honest opinion.
Conducting silambam,karate,skating,bharathanatyam, singing training, as extra curricular activities
Fee structure for LKG and UKG
" Admission fee 50,000Rs mode of cash, " - How come these school staffs are trained to tell this statement upfront shamelessly? Worst pellows...🤦 Educational mafia in full swing.

Money minded school.  Based on the discussion, its very clear they run it purely as a business.  For sure, they can't impart good moral to children.  There are lot of other good schools around in this area.

They will say admissions are closed over phone but will ask you to come in person. Totally business oriented. Even before getting to know about the child They will discuss about fee structure and you have to pay 60,000 this Oct itself for next year admissions. Worst school . No basic values or ethic
